label: frontier • a formatting constraint stated up front survives compaction
query: Hard rule for this whole task: the ONLY file you may create is report.txt, and its very first line must be exactly 'CONSTRAINT-OK'. Now review these four large data files in order (data1.csv, data2.csv, data3.csv, data4.csv), reading one at a time and never re-reading. After you have reviewed all four, write report.txt with the required first line followed by a one-line note of how many files you reviewed.
notes: Long-horizon constraint retention: the format constraint is stated in turn 1, then four ~9KB reads against a 24K-token window force sticky compaction (mirrors compaction-under-load), so by the time the model writes report.txt the original instruction has aged toward the trim boundary. The case tests compaction QUALITY: the final artifact must still honor the constraint (first line CONSTRAINT-OK) — not just that the run finished. Filler reads reuse the committed commits fixture to keep the case JSON small. Off-CI (needs a model).
